content: I walked into the interview expecting the usual nervous small talk. Instead, the interviewer barely looked up from his phone. Fifteen minutes late, he started with, “So, why do you want this job?” Then he spent ten minutes complaining about his last employee before asking me to solve a problem on a whiteboard—without a marker. When I finally borrowed one, the projector flickered out, so he gave up and just stared at me. Midway, a phone call interrupted him; he answered loudly, discussing weekend plans while I sat there, silent. At the end, he said, “You’re... interesting. We’ll be in touch,” then disappeared without a handshake. Outside, I wondered if I’d just sat through some elaborate prank or if this was actually how interviews went now. Either way, I decided the “worst interview” label wasn’t overhyped—it just came with a healthy dose of disbelief.
